one_time_description: CMHC Rapid Housing Grant received by SLFN Housing Authority - non-recurring capital funding
economic_notes: COVID-19 caused significant decline in VLT revenues and slight decline in cabin rental revenues. Operations include VLT lounge, gas bar, RV park, log cabins, farm, potato chip plant, wind farm, and Headingley building (commercial real estate). SLFN Enterprises operates ATM/VLT, term deposits, and various enterprise activities.
notes: Six fully consolidated subsidiaries: SLFN Enterprises (100%), Swan Lake First Nation Housing Authority (100%), Swan Lake First Nation Gaming Commission (100%), Swan Lake First Nation Farm (100%), 6226124 Manitoba Ltd. (100%), SLFN Property Management Ltd. (100%). Swan Lake First Nation Farm has a November 30 year-end; transactions Dec 1 - Mar 31 flow through band accounts. Large RBC loans on Headingley building ($18.6M combined) are classified mostly as current (due within one year = $21M), creating significant current portion of long-term debt. total_government_transfers estimated as ISC ($9,792,463) + FNIHB ($3,608,086) + Provincial funding ($893,612) = $14,294,161. Segmented revenue total ($24,229,444) differs from statement of operations total ($26,111,522) due to eliminations and presentation differences. Payroll note discloses $3,188,531 in eligible salaries for ISC-funded employees only, not total salaries. No schedule of remuneration provided.
